title: hieracium pilosum subsp. leucopogon
intro: Hieracium pilosum subsp. leucopogon, also known as the woolly hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is native to Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium pilosum subsp. leucopogon include reducing inflammation, soothing wounds, and promoting skin health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health issues.
